name: cool_all_day_heat_all_day
description: Cold day with high of 58F — indoor starts at 68F, below comfort_heat=70F. Cold classification sets heat mode. Heat applies at comfort_heat=70F. No nat vent window (outdoor too cold). Bedtime setback applies at night.
notes: Covers the standard cold day pattern: heat on all day, no nat vent, setback at night.
Outdoor never reaches indoor levels — no nat vent activation is possible.
Bedtime setback uses default depth (4F below comfort_heat) since no thermal model is configured.
This is a gap-filling scenario: confirms heat mode is applied and maintained, setback fires correctly.

events.note: Cold winter morning: indoor 68F (below comfort_heat=70F), outdoor 38F.
Mid-morning: heat has brought indoor to 70.5F. Outdoor still 42F — no nat vent.

Afternoon: outdoor reached 56F (peak for the day). Still below indoor 70F — nat vent activation guard: outdoor < indoor but indoor exactly at comfort_heat=70F (not above), so nat vent will not activate if door opens.
Bedtime: heat setback fires. Default depth = comfort_heat(70) - 4F = 66F. Floor is setback_heat=62F so 66F is above floor.

assertions.reason: No new event at 9 AM that changes state — last decision was classification_applied at 6:01 AM. Temp update with nat vent inactive and no sensors open produces no new decision.
Bedtime setback: heat mode, default depth 4F below comfort_heat=70F → 66F. Above setback_heat=62F floor.

verdict.summary: Cold day applies heat at comfort target all day; bedtime setback fires at default depth
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (heat, 70F) at 6 AM → setback_applied (66F) at bedtime
verdict.expected_behavior: Continuous heat mode all day; no nat vent (outdoor too cold); bedtime setback to 66F
